title: use-after-free in ngx.pipe when a QUIC connection closes and destroys the pool first
category: use-after-free (pool destroyed before pipe cleanup handlers run; Lua ngx.pipe ↔ C nginx pool)
crash_site: nginx segfault in a pipe stdout-read cleanup handler (ngx_http_lua_pipe_proc_read_stdout_cleanup, src/ngx_http_lua_pipe.c), invoked during connection teardown. The cleanup runs against memory already freed with the connection's pool.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the *_cleanup handlers where it crashes — ngx_http_lua_pipe_proc_read_stdout_cleanup / _read_stderr_cleanup / _write_cleanup / _wait_cleanup. They are the crash sites; the defect is the destroy/close ordering relative to pool destruction.
ground_truth.fix_summary: On the QUIC connection-close path the connection pool is destroyed, running the pipe's pending cleanup handlers against already-freed data -> UAF. Fix: in ngx_http_lua_ffi_pipe_proc_destroy ensure the pipe's connections/cleanups are closed before pool destruction (add a helper that tears them down deterministically).

admission.note: Authored from PR #2483 body+gdb bt. crash != cause: gdb frame #0 is ngx_http_lua_pipe_proc_read_stdout_cleanup (a cleanup handler) run during pool destruction on QUIC close; the cause is the destroy/close ordering owned by ngx_http_lua_ffi_pipe_proc_destroy. Decoys = the four *_cleanup handlers. Lua ngx.pipe lifetime <-> C nginx pool. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/openresty/lua-nginx-module/pull/2483 — prevent use-after-free crash in ngx_http_lua_pipe (QUIC close path); fix cac9cbc3b700, base a44c67f41876.
